root_text.sn45.27:1.2: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kumbho anādhāro suppavattiyo hoti, sādhāro duppavattiyo hoti;
root_text.sn45.27:1.3: evameva kho, bhikkhave, cittaṁ anādhāraṁ suppavattiyaṁ hoti, sādhāraṁ duppavattiyaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn45.27:1.8: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, kumbho anādhāro suppavattiyo hoti, sādhāro duppavattiyo hoti;
root_text.sn45.27:1.9: evameva kho, bhikkhave, cittaṁ anādhāraṁ suppavattiyaṁ hoti, sādhāraṁ duppavattiyaṁ hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn45.27:1.2: “A pot without a stand is easy to overturn, but if it has a stand it’s hard to overturn.
translation_text.sn45.27:1.3: In the same way, a mind without a stand is easy to overturn, but if it has a stand it’s hard to overturn.
translation_text.sn45.27:1.4: And what’s the stand for the mind?
translation_text.sn45.27:1.5: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn45.27:1.6: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.27:1.7: This is the stand for the mind.
translation_text.sn45.27:1.8: A pot without a stand is easy to overturn, but if it has a stand it’s hard to overturn.
translation_text.sn45.27:1.9: In the same way, a mind without a stand is easy to overturn, but if it has a stand it’s hard to overturn.”

reference_text.sn45.27:1.1: cck19.22, csp1ed14.17, csp2ed14.17, ms14S5_106, msdiv27, pts-vp-pli5.21, vri27.20
